# SalaryTakeHome.co.uk

> Free UK tax and salary calculators using official HMRC 2026/27 tax rates. All calculations run client-side in the browser — no data is stored or transmitted.

## About

SalaryTakeHome.co.uk provides accurate, up-to-date UK tax calculators for employees, job seekers, and anyone who wants to understand their pay. Tax data is updated each April when new HMRC rates take effect. The site covers income tax, National Insurance, student loan repayments, pension contributions, Scottish tax rates, and more.

## Calculators

- [Take-Home Pay Calculator](https://www.salarytakehome.co.uk/take-home-pay-calculator): Calculate your exact take-home pay after income tax, National Insurance, student loans, and pension deductions. Supports tax years 2021/22 through 2026/27, Scottish tax rates, and all student loan plan types.
- [Pay Rise Calculator](https://www.salarytakehome.co.uk/pay-rise): See how a pay rise affects your take-home pay after tax. Compare before and after deductions.
- [Hourly Wage Calculator](https://www.salarytakehome.co.uk/hourly-wage): Convert an hourly wage to annual salary and calculate take-home pay with full tax breakdown.
- [Compare Salaries](https://www.salarytakehome.co.uk/compare-salaries): Compare two salaries side by side with full tax, NI, and student loan breakdowns.
- [Two Jobs Calculator](https://www.salarytakehome.co.uk/two-jobs): Calculate combined take-home pay when working two jobs, with correct tax code handling.
- [Pro-Rata Calculator](https://www.salarytakehome.co.uk/pro-rata-furlough): Calculate part-time or pro-rata salary and take-home pay.
- [Sick Pay Calculator](https://www.salarytakehome.co.uk/sick-pay): Calculate Statutory Sick Pay (SSP) entitlement and duration.
- [Maternity Pay Calculator](https://www.salarytakehome.co.uk/maternity-sick-pay): Calculate Statutory Maternity Pay (SMP) week by week.
- [Mortgage Calculator](https://www.salarytakehome.co.uk/mortgages): Calculate monthly mortgage repayments with interest rate comparisons.
- [Debt Repayment Calculator](https://www.salarytakehome.co.uk/debts): Plan debt repayment with avalanche and snowball method comparisons.
- [Tax Code Checker](https://www.salarytakehome.co.uk/tax-code): Enter your UK tax code to find out what it means, your Personal Allowance, which tax rates apply, and whether it's correct.
- [Salary Per Second](https://www.salarytakehome.co.uk/salary-per-second): Convert any salary into per year, month, week, day, hour, minute, and second. Includes a live earnings counter and fun comparisons.
- [Dividend Tax Calculator](https://www.salarytakehome.co.uk/dividend-tax): Calculate dividend tax based on your salary and dividend income. Shows tax at basic (10.75% from April 2026, previously 8.75%), higher (35.75% from April 2026, previously 33.75%), and additional (39.35%) rates, with dividend allowance and Personal Allowance tapering. Supports tax years 2021/22 to 2026/27.

## UK Tax Reference (2026/27)

- Personal Allowance: £12,570 (frozen until April 2031, Autumn Budget 2025)
- Basic rate (20%): £12,571 to £50,270
- Higher rate (40%): £50,271 to £125,140
- Additional rate (45%): over £125,140
- National Insurance: 8% on earnings between £12,570 and £50,270, 2% above £50,270
- Scottish rates (2026/27 bands): Starter 19% to £16,537, Basic 20% to £29,526, Intermediate 21% to £43,662, Higher 42% to £75,000, Advanced 45% to £125,140, Top 48% above £125,140
- Student Loan Plan 1 threshold: £26,900 (9%)
- Student Loan Plan 2 threshold: £29,385 (9%)
- Student Loan Plan 4 threshold: £33,795 (9%)
- Student Loan Plan 5 threshold: £25,000 (9%, repayments commenced April 2026)
- Postgraduate Loan threshold: £21,000 (6%)
- Dividend allowance: £500; dividend tax rates 10.75% / 35.75% / 39.35% (basic and higher rates increased by 2pp from April 2026)
- Statutory Sick Pay: £123.25/week or 80% of average weekly earnings (whichever is lower); no Lower Earnings Limit; paid from day 1 (reforms in force from 6 April 2026)
- Statutory Maternity Pay: 90% of AWE for 6 weeks, then £194.32/week or 90% of AWE for up to 33 weeks; qualifying threshold £129/week
- National Living Wage (age 21+): £12.71/hour (from 1 April 2026, up 4.1% from £12.21)
- ISA allowance: £20,000 (unchanged)

## Blog

- [UK Tax Codes Explained](https://www.salarytakehome.co.uk/blog/understanding-uk-tax-code): Complete guide to UK tax codes — what each letter and number means, common codes (1257L, BR, D0, K), Scottish S prefix, emergency codes, and how to check yours.
- [Scottish Tax vs English Tax](https://www.salarytakehome.co.uk/blog/scottish-tax-vs-english-tax): Detailed comparison of Scottish and English/Welsh tax bands and rates.
- [Plan 1 vs Plan 2 Student Loan](https://www.salarytakehome.co.uk/blog/plan-1-vs-plan-2-student-loan): Differences between student loan repayment plans explained.
- [Salary Sacrifice vs Personal Pension](https://www.salarytakehome.co.uk/blog/salary-sacrifice-vs-personal-pension): Which pension contribution method saves you more tax.
- [£100K Tax Trap Explained](https://www.salarytakehome.co.uk/blog/100k-tax-trap): How earning over £100,000 creates a 60% effective tax rate.
- [Take-Home Pay on £20K](https://www.salarytakehome.co.uk/blog/20k-salary-take-home): Full breakdown of take-home pay on a £20,000 salary.
- [Take-Home Pay on £25K](https://www.salarytakehome.co.uk/blog/25k-salary-take-home): Full breakdown of take-home pay on a £25,000 salary.

- [Take-Home Pay on £90K](https://www.salarytakehome.co.uk/blog/90k-salary-take-home): Full breakdown of take-home pay on a £90,000 salary.

- [UK Tax Changes 2026/27](https://www.salarytakehome.co.uk/blog/uk-tax-changes-2026-27): Complete guide to every UK tax change from 6 April 2026 — frozen thresholds extended to 2031, Scottish band changes, +2pp dividend tax rise, SSP reform, new student loan thresholds.
- [UK Tax Changes 2025/26](https://www.salarytakehome.co.uk/blog/uk-tax-changes-2025-26): Historical reference — all tax changes for the 2025/26 tax year.

- [Salary Sacrifice Complete Guide](https://www.salarytakehome.co.uk/blog/salary-sacrifice-complete-guide): How salary sacrifice works and its tax benefits.
- [Pension Contributions & Tax Relief](https://www.salarytakehome.co.uk/blog/pension-contributions-tax-relief): Guide to pension tax relief and contribution limits.
- [Pay Rise Negotiation Guide](https://www.salarytakehome.co.uk/blog/pay-rise-negotiation-guide): How to negotiate a pay rise and understand its tax impact.
- [Student Loan Refund Guide](https://www.salarytakehome.co.uk/blog/student-loan-refund): How to claim back student loan overpayments.
- [Cash ISA Limit Changes](https://www.salarytakehome.co.uk/blog/cash-isa-limit-2027): Upcoming changes to ISA contribution limits.
- [UK Minimum Wage 2026/27](https://www.salarytakehome.co.uk/blog/uk-minimum-wage-2026-27): Complete guide to UK minimum wage rates from 1 April 2026 — £12.71 NLW, rates by age band, take-home pay.
- [UK Minimum Wage 2025/26](https://www.salarytakehome.co.uk/blog/uk-minimum-wage-2025-26): Historical reference — minimum wage rates in force from April 2025.
- [How to Compare Job Offers](https://www.salarytakehome.co.uk/blog/how-to-compare-job-offers): Learn how to compare job offers beyond just salary.
- [Two Jobs Tax Explained](https://www.salarytakehome.co.uk/blog/two-jobs-tax-explained): How PAYE works when you have multiple employers in the UK.
- [Pro-Rata Salary Explained](https://www.salarytakehome.co.uk/blog/pro-rata-salary-explained): How pro-rata salary works for part-time workers in the UK.
- [SSP Changes 2026](https://www.salarytakehome.co.uk/blog/ssp-changes-2026): What the new sick pay rules mean for you.
- [Maternity Pay Guide](https://www.salarytakehome.co.uk/blog/maternity-pay-guide): SMP, Maternity Allowance and Shared Parental Leave explained.
- [First-Time Buyer Mortgage Guide](https://www.salarytakehome.co.uk/blog/first-time-buyer-mortgage-guide): Deposits, government schemes, stamp duty relief and how to get your first mortgage.
- [How to Pay Off Debt Faster](https://www.salarytakehome.co.uk/blog/how-to-pay-off-debt-faster): Snowball vs avalanche debt repayment strategies for the UK.
- [Cash ISA vs Lifetime ISA](https://www.salarytakehome.co.uk/blog/isa-vs-lisa): Compare Cash ISAs and LISAs — bonus, penalties, and which suits your goals.
- [PAYE vs Self-Employed Tax](https://www.salarytakehome.co.uk/blog/paye-vs-self-employed): Key differences between employed and self-employed tax in the UK.
- [Full-Time vs Part-Time Tax](https://www.salarytakehome.co.uk/blog/full-time-vs-part-time-tax): How working hours affect your tax bands, NI, and take-home pay.
- [UK Dividend Tax Explained](https://www.salarytakehome.co.uk/blog/uk-dividend-tax-explained): Complete guide to UK dividend tax rates, allowances, worked examples, and strategies to reduce your bill.
